questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x=\frac{p}{q-r}, y=\frac{q}{r-p}, z=\frac{r}{p-q}$, then $(x y+y z+z x+1)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& x=\frac{p}{q-r} \cdot y=\frac{q}{r-p} \cdot z=\frac{r}{p-q} \\
& \Rightarrow p-x q+x r=0....(i) \\
& y p+q-y r=0....(ii) \\
& z p-z q-r=0.....(iii)
\end{aligned}$

This homogeneous system of equations in $p, q, r$ can't have trivial solution, i.e. $p \neq q \neq r \neq 0$ as it will make $x, y, z$ not defined.
Hence determinant of coefficients should be zero.
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { i.e. }\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
1 & -x & x \\
y & 1 & -y \\
z & -z & -1
\end{array}\right|=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x y+y z+z x+1=0
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If $0 \leq[x] \lt 2,-1 \leq[y] \lt 1$ and $1 \leq[z] \lt 3$, (where [.] denotes the greatest integer function) then the maximum value of the determinant $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{[\mathrm{x}]+1} & {[\mathrm{y}]} & {[\mathrm{z}]} \\ {[\mathrm{x}]} & {[\mathrm{y}]+1} & {[\mathrm{z}]} \\ {[\mathrm{x}]} & {[\mathrm{y}]} & {[\mathrm{z}]+1}\end{array}\right|$ is _______
questions.solution: $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 & -1 \\ {[x]} & {[y]} & {[z]+1}\end{array}\right| R_1 \rightarrow R_1-R_3, R_2 \rightarrow R_2-R_3$ on solving $[\mathrm{x}]+[\mathrm{y}]+[\mathrm{z}]+1$, taking maximum value, we get 4

If for these values of ' $a$ ' and ' $\theta$ '
$=\frac{p}{q}$where $p, q \in N$, then find least value of $|p-4 q|$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { If }\left(x_1-x_2\right)^2+\left(y_1-y_2\right)^2=a^2 \\ & \left(x_2-x_3\right)^2+\left(y_2-y_3\right)^2=b^2 \\ & \text { and }\left(x_3-x_1\right)^2+\left(y_3-y_1\right)^2=c^2\end{aligned}$
then $\lambda\left|\begin{array}{lll}x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1\end{array}\right|^2=(a+b+c)(b+c-a)(c+a-b)(a+b-c)$. Find the value of $\lambda$.
questions.solution: First 3 equations are suggestive that $\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1\right)\left(\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2\right)$ and $\left(\mathrm{x}_3, \mathrm{y}_3\right)$ and the vertices of a $\triangle \mathrm{BCA}$ Now $\left.A=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{x}_1 & \mathrm{y}_1 & 1 \\ \mathrm{x}_2 & \mathrm{y}_2 & 1 \\ \mathrm{x}_3 & \mathrm{y}_3 & 1\end{array}\right| \right\rvert\,$ $\therefore \quad 4 \mathrm{~A}^2=\left|\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{x}_1 & \mathrm{y}_1 & 1 \\ \mathrm{x}_2 & \mathrm{y}_2 & 1 \\ \mathrm{x}_3 & \mathrm{y}_3 & 1\end{array}\right|^2$ $\begin{aligned} & 4 A^2=4 s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c) \\ & 16 A^2=2 s(2 s-2 a)(2 s-2 b)(2 s-2 c)=R H S\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and LHS }=(16)\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)\left|\begin{array}{lll}x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1\end{array}\right|^2 \\ & \Rightarrow 4\left|\begin{array}{lll}x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1\end{array}\right|^2\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \lambda=4$ Ans.]
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a_1, a_2, a_3, 5,4, a_6, a_7, a_8, a_9$ are in H.P., then the value of the determinant $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}a_1 & a_2 & a_3 \\ 5 & 4 & a_6 \\ a_7 & a_8 & a_9\end{array}\right|$ can be expressed in the lowest form as $\frac{p}{q}$, find $(p+q)$.
questions.solution:
We have $D=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}a_1 & a_2 & a_3 \\ 5 & 4 & a_6 \\ a_7 & a_8 & a_9\end{array}\right|$
Since $a_n=\frac{20}{n} ; d=\frac{1}{20}$
Hence $D=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}20 & \frac{20}{2} & \frac{20}{3} \\ \frac{20}{4} & \frac{20}{5} & \frac{20}{6} \\ \frac{20}{7} & \frac{20}{8} & \frac{20}{9}\end{array}\right|=\frac{(20)^3}{4 \times 7}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & \frac{1}{2} & \frac{1}{3} \\ 1 & \frac{4}{5} & \frac{2}{3} \\ 1 & \frac{7}{8} & \frac{7}{9}\end{array}\right|$
$R_1 \rightarrow R_1-R_2$ and $R_2 \rightarrow R_2-R_3$
$=\frac{(20)^3}{4 \times 7}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}0 & \frac{-3}{10} & \frac{-1}{3} \\ 0 & \frac{-3}{40} & \frac{-1}{9} \\ 1 & \frac{7}{8} & \frac{7}{9}\end{array}\right|=\frac{50}{21}=\frac{p}{q}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow p=50, q=21 \\ & \Rightarrow(p+q)=71 \text { Ans. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}a+b+2 c & a & b \\ c & b+c+2 a & b \\ c & a & c+a+2 b\end{array}\right|=k(\alpha a+\beta b+\gamma c)^3$, then $(2 \alpha+\beta-\gamma)^k$ is $\left(\alpha, \beta, \gamma, k \in z^{+}\right)$
questions.solution:
LHS $=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}a+b+2 c & a & b \\ c & b+c+2 a & b \\ c & a & c+a+2 b\end{array}\right|$
Operate: $\mathrm{C}_1 \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{C}_3$
$=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2(a+b+c) & a & b \\ 2(a+b+c) & b+c+2 a & b \\ 2(a+b+c) & a & c+a+2 b\end{array}\right|=2(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & a & b \\ 1 & b+c+2 a & b \\ 1 & a & c+a+2 b\end{array}\right|$
Operate : $R_2 \rightarrow R_2-R_1 ; R_3 \rightarrow R_3-R_1$
$=2(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & a & b \\ 0 & (a+b+c) & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & (c+a+b)\end{array}\right|$

Expand by $\mathrm{C}_1$
$=2(a+b+c) .1 \cdot\left|\begin{array}{cc}a+b+c & 0 \\ 0 & a+b+c\end{array}\right|=2(a+b+c)^3 \Rightarrow k=2$ and $\alpha=\beta=\gamma=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left|\begin{array}{lll}b+c & c+a & a+b \\ c+a & a+b & b+c \\ a+b & b+c & c+a\end{array}\right| \geq 0$, where $a, b, c \in R^{+}-\{0\}$, then $\frac{a+b}{c}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\Delta=\left|\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c} & \mathrm{c}+\mathrm{a} & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b} \\ \mathrm{c}+\mathrm{a} & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b} & \mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c} \\ \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b} & \mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c} & \mathrm{c}+\mathrm{a}\end{array}\right|$ Operate : $\mathrm{C}_1 \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{C}_3$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \Delta=\left|\begin{array}{lll}2(a+b+c) & c+a & a+b \\ 2(a+b+a) & a+b & b+c \\ 2(a+b+c) & b+c & c+a\end{array}\right|=2(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{lll}1 & c+a & a+b \\ 1 & a+b & b+c \\ 1 & b+c & c+a\end{array}\right| \text { Operate: } R_2 \rightarrow R_2-R_1 ; R_3 \rightarrow R_3-R_1 \\ & \Rightarrow D=2(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{lll}1 & c+a & a+b \\ 0 & b-c & c-a \\ 0 & b-a & c-b\end{array}\right| \\ & =2(a+b+c) 1 .\left|\begin{array}{ll}b-c & c-a \\ b-a & c-b\end{array}\right| \text { open w.r.t. } R_1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =2(a+b+c)[(b-c)(c-b)-(c-a)(b-a)]=2(a+b+c)\left[b c-b^2-c^2+c b-\left(c b-a c-a b+a^2\right)\right] \\ & =2(a+b+c)\left(a b+b c+c a-a^2-b^2-c^2\right) \leq 0 \quad \Rightarrow a=b=c\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
